source_report: deep-research-report.md (Founder Intent Filter for Sandbox-First Autonomy)
downscope_decision: Implemented lightweight repo-local enforcement only. Did NOT introduce OPA, Kubernetes admission, Firecracker, gVisor, SLSA, or Sigstore, per the report's own downscope recommendation and the current repo's actual stack.

rejected_examples.example: Sina must approve the release manually.
All feature branches require Sina merges into main.
L5 review happens for every run regardless of risk level.
the response is: denied. (no repair/reroute keyword nearby)
Zero-drift required before proceeding, regardless of feature scope.

self_scan_result: PASS - repo's own governance files, once fixtures/canon are correctly scoped, contain zero active violations
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_real_verific: Exclude-glob pattern '**/tests/fixtures/**' did not match paths without a leading segment (fnmatch has no true recursive-glob support) - fixed to use root-relative patterns.
Canon file itself (FOUNDER_INTENT_FILTER.md) tripped its own R5 pattern because it quotes the violation language as a documented example - added explicit self-exclusion for canon/policy files.
